openmeter/currencies/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing currencies.Adapter for custom currencies and cost bases. All DB access is transaction-aware via entutils.TransactingRepo; the adapter struct implements the TxCreator+TxUser triad required by entutils.TransactingRepo to start or join caller-supplied transactions.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping every method
patterns.description: Every public adapter method body is wrapped with entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx, tx) ...) — never call a.db directly without this wrapper so the ctx-bound Ent transaction is honoured.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (currencies.Currency, error) { curr, err := tx.db.CustomCurrency.Create()... })

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self triad
patterns.description: The adapter implements TxCreator: Tx() hijacks a new pg transaction via a.db.HijackTx, WithTx() rebinds the adapter to an existing TxDriver using entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig, Self() returns itself. All three are required for TransactingRepo.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) Tx(ctx context.Context) (context.Context, transaction.Driver, error) { txCtx, rawConfig, eDriver, err := a.db.HijackTx(ctx, &sql.TxOptions{ReadOnly: false}); return txCtx, entutils.NewTxDriver(eDriver, rawConfig), nil }

patterns.name: Config struct + Validate + New constructor
patterns.description: Adapter is constructed via New(Config) with Config.Validate() guard; callers never instantiate the adapter struct directly.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (currencies.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &adapter{db: config.Client}, nil }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Declare var _ currencies.Adapter = (*adapter)(nil) in both adapter.go and currencies.go to catch interface drift at compile time.

patterns.name: entdb.IsConstraintError → GenericConflictError
patterns.description: On Ent constraint violations wrap with models.NewGenericConflictError so the HTTP layer maps it to 409 automatically.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsConstraintError(err) { return currencies.Currency{}, models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("currency with code %s already exists", params.Code)) }

patterns.name: mapXFromDB mapper functions
patterns.description: DB row → domain type conversion is always in package-level mapXFromDB functions (mapCurrencyFromDB, mapCostBasisFromDB); never inline struct construction in query handlers.
patterns.example: func mapCurrencyFromDB(c *entdb.CustomCurrency) currencies.Currency { return currencies.Currency{NamespacedID: models.NamespacedID{ID: c.ID, Namespace: c.Namespace}, Code: c.Code, ...} }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, New constructor, the adapter struct, and the Tx/WithTx/Self transaction plumbing — the skeleton every new adapter method depends on.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic here; it is strictly wiring. If Self() or WithTx() is removed or signature changes, TransactingRepo breaks silently.

key_file_guides.role: Implements all currencies.Adapter methods (ListCustomCurrencies, CreateCurrency, CreateCostBasis, ListCostBases) plus the DB→domain mappers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListCustomCurrencies uses manual Offset/Limit pagination; ListCostBases uses the generated q.Paginate helper — keep both consistent when extending. Mapper functions must normalize time to UTC (c.EffectiveFrom.In(time.UTC)).

anti_patterns: Calling a.db.X directly inside a method body without entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses the ctx-bound Ent transaction and produces partial writes
Adding service-layer validation or business logic inside the adapter — belongs in openmeter/currencies/service
Constructing *adapter directly instead of calling New(Config)
Returning raw Ent errors to callers — always wrap or map to models.Generic* errors
Storing *entdb.Tx as a struct field instead of using the TxDriver/TransactingRepo pattern

decisions.decision: Tx/WithTx/Self triad inlined on the adapter struct rather than a separate TxWrapper type
decisions.rationale: entutils.TransactingRepo requires the repository itself to implement TxCreator; inlining the triad avoids an extra indirection layer and matches the pattern used across all other domain adapters.

decisions.decision: Separate mapper functions (mapCurrencyFromDB, mapCostBasisFromDB) rather than methods on Ent types
decisions.rationale: Keeps generated Ent types isolated from domain types; changes to Ent schema only require updating the mapper, not every call site.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new adapter method that writes to the DB

openmeter/currencies/service.purpose: Business-logic layer for currencies and cost bases. Validates inputs, resolves fiat vs. custom currency enumeration in-memory from GOBL, and delegates persistence to currencies.Adapter via transaction.Run.

patterns.name: Input validation before transaction
patterns.description: Every public method calls params.Validate() and returns models.NewGenericValidationError if it fails — before opening a transaction.
patterns.example: if params.Validate() != nil { return pagination.Result[currencies.Currency]{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("invalid input parameters: %w", params.Validate())) }

patterns.name: transaction.Run wrapping adapter calls
patterns.description: All adapter interactions are wrapped in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx) ...) so the service participates in caller-supplied transactions.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (currencies.Currency, error) { return s.adapter.CreateCurrency(ctx, params) })

patterns.name: In-memory fiat enumeration via GOBL
patterns.description: Fiat currencies come from currency.Definitions() (invopop/gobl) filtered to ISO-numeric-only; custom currencies come from the adapter. Combined listing merges both slices and applies manual pagination in-memory.
patterns.example: filteredMatchCode, err := lo.FilterErr(currency.Definitions(), func(def *currency.Def, _ int) (bool, error) { if def.ISONumeric == "" { return false, nil }; return matchCode(def.ISOCode.String(), 0) })

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: var _ currencies.CurrencyService = (*Service)(nil) at package top ensures Service always satisfies the interface.

patterns.name: Business-logic only — no Ent imports
patterns.description: The service package must not import openmeter/ent/db; all DB access must flow through currencies.Adapter to preserve the layer boundary.
patterns.example: // service.go imports only: currencies, transaction, models, pagination, gobl, samber/lo — never openmeter/ent/db

key_file_guides.role: Single file implementing currencies.CurrencyService: ListCurrencies (merges fiat+custom in-memory), CreateCurrency, CreateCostBasis (enforces future-only EffectiveFrom with default to time.Now()), ListCostBases.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListCurrencies has two distinct pagination paths — DB-level (custom-only fast path) vs. in-memory (fiat or combined); adding a third filter type must handle both correctly. EffectiveFrom defaulting to time.Now() when nil is service-layer business logic, not adapter logic.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests using a fakeAdapter (implements currencies.Adapter with in-memory slice) and noopDriver (transaction.Driver no-op). Tests cover combined/custom-only listing paths, filter operators (Eq, In, Ne), sort order, and validation errors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use t.Context() (not context.Background()). fakeAdapter.ListCustomCurrencies applies Code filter to simulate DB-level filtering — new adapter methods added to the interface must be added as panicking stubs here.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/ent/db directly — all DB access must go through the currencies.Adapter interface
Skipping params.Validate() before calling the adapter — constraint errors from the DB are harder to diagnose than early validation errors
Applying pagination logic inside the adapter for combined fiat+custom queries — the adapter only handles DB rows; in-memory merging and pagination belong here
Using context.Background() instead of propagating the caller's ctx through transaction.Run
Adding temporal business rules (future-date enforcement, defaulting) inside the adapter — belongs in the service

decisions.decision: Fiat currency list sourced in-memory from GOBL, not stored in the DB
decisions.rationale: ISO fiat currencies are stable and exhaustive in the GOBL library; storing them in Postgres would require synchronisation and add write paths with no benefit.

decisions.decision: EffectiveFrom defaulting and future-date validation lives in the service, not the adapter
decisions.rationale: Adapter is a pure persistence layer; temporal business rules must be enforced regardless of which code path calls the adapter, so they belong in the service.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new service method that validates input and delegates to the adapter inside a transaction
